extra.reasoning: The input `syncope` does not appear in any rule (only r1 references `vomiting`, r2 references `sweating`) and does not affect the default. Therefore acuity is completely independent of syncope. For every fixed (sweating, vomiting) pair, toggling syncope from false to true leaves acuity unchanged (equal), which satisfies "no lower than before." Enumerating the relevant inputs: (sw=F,vom=F)->routine (default), (sw=F,vom=T)->urgent (r1), (sw=T,vom=F)->urgent (r2), (sw=T,vom=T)->urgent (both r1,r2 fire, same output). In all cases acuity is identical regardless of syncope's value, so monotonicity holds across the entire input space.
